name: story-agent-vscode
description: Agentic story delivery — Aha integration, observation lounge wizard, and story lifecycle management inside VS Code

chatParticipants.name: story-agent
chatParticipants.description: Agentic story delivery — prepare Aha stories, track PRs, and manage the delivery lifecycle

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Chat with the token-optimizing assistant (model tiering, caching, RAG context pruning)

commands.name: agent
commands.description: Autonomous agentic mode — the crew reads/edits files & runs commands via agent-core (WorfGate-governed, OpenRouter cost-optimized)

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Plan mode — the crew proposes an ordered plan for a task WITHOUT editing files (review before executing)

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review the current working changes (git diff) for bugs, security, and clarity — read-only

commands.name: symphony
commands.description: Show the live system posture — firm → client → project hierarchy, WorfGate credentials/providers, and tool-theory coverage

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset the session token budget meter

commands.name: prepare
commands.description: Load an Aha story and generate an execution brief for human review (Observation Lounge)

commands.name: status
commands.description: Check current status of a tracked story

commands.name: dashboard
commands.description: Open the Story Agent web dashboard

commands.title: Story Agent: Review Changes (accept/reject per file)
Story Agent: Inline Chat (ask about selection)
Story Agent: Connect OAuth Provider (Aha!, Google, AWS…)

storyAgent.ahaDomain.description: Aha domain (e.g. yourcompany.aha.io). Overridden by AHA_DOMAIN env var.

storyAgent.ahaApiKey.description: Aha API key. Overridden by AHA_API_KEY env var. Prefer storing in environment.

storyAgent.dashboardUrl.description: Base URL of the Story Agent Next.js dashboard.

enumDescriptions: Jira Cloud — requires JIRA_DOMAIN, JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN
Linear — requires LINEAR_API_KEY (stub — not yet implemented)
GitHub Projects v2 — requires GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_ORG (stub)
Azure DevOps — requires AZURE_DEVOPS_ORG and AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN (stub)
storyAgent.agileProvider.description: Agile project management provider to use for story intake.

enumDescriptions: OpenRouter (cost-optimized crew routing) first, Copilot only as fallback — recommended
Force OpenRouter (errors if no key configured)
Force VS Code Copilot (uses your subscription; ignores crew cost routing)
storyAgent.chat.provider.description: Which LLM backend the chat assistant uses. 'auto' prioritizes OpenRouter + Quark cost-optimized routing, falling back to Copilot.

storyAgent.chat.tokenBudget.description: Per-session token budget for the chat assistant. The live meter warns as you approach it and blocks further turns once reached (use /reset to clear).

storyAgent.chat.modelTiering.description: Quark cost-optimized routing: simple turns use the cheap model, complex turns the quality model. Disable to always use the quality model.

storyAgent.chat.costProfile.description: Routing profile. 'cost_optimized' (default) tiers cheap vs quality by task complexity; 'quality_first' always uses the quality model. Falls back to the CREW_LLM_MODEL_PROFILE env var if set.

storyAgent.chat.openRouterApiKey.description: OpenRouter API key (PRIMARY backend). Prefer the crew env var CREW_LLM_APPROVED_KEY (auto-loaded from ~/.alexai-secrets); this setting is a fallback for when VS Code is not launched from a shell with that env.

storyAgent.chat.openRouterUrl.description: OpenRouter base URL. Falls back to the CREW_LLM_APPROVED_URL env var if set.

storyAgent.chat.openRouterModel.description: Quality model slug (complex/critical turns). Falls back to the CREW_LLM_APPROVED_MODEL env var if set.

storyAgent.chat.openRouterModelCheap.description: Cost-optimized model slug (simple/support turns). Falls back to the CREW_LLM_APPROVED_MODEL_CHEAP env var if set.

storyAgent.chat.smallModelFamily.description: Copilot-fallback model family used for simple turns.

storyAgent.chat.capableModelFamily.description: Copilot-fallback model family used for complex turns.

storyAgent.chat.ragTopK.description: Max number of editor/workspace context snippets injected per turn (RAG context pruning). Lower = fewer input tokens.

storyAgent.chat.cacheTtlMinutes.description: How long identical prompt+context responses are cached (minutes). Cache hits cost zero tokens.

storyAgent.chat.ragUseCloudMemory.description: Augment chat with the crew's cloud RAG memory (observation memories + documentation) via the RAG read service. Falls back gracefully to editor-only context if the service is unavailable.

storyAgent.chat.ragServiceUrl.description: URL of the Story Agent RAG read service (scripts/start-rag-service.ts). Provides crew cloud memory to the assistant without the extension holding the service_role key.

storyAgent.chat.ragServiceToken.description: Optional bearer token for the RAG read service (matches RAG_SERVICE_TOKEN). Falls back to the RAG_SERVICE_TOKEN env var.

storyAgent.chat.agentServiceUrl.description: URL of the agent-core autonomous loop endpoint (MCP server started with STORY_AGENT_AGENT_PORT). Used by the /agent command. Falls back to the STORY_AGENT_AGENT_URL env var.

storyAgent.chat.agentServiceToken.description: Optional bearer token for the agent-core endpoint (matches AGENT_SERVICE_TOKEN). Falls back to the AGENT_SERVICE_TOKEN env var.

storyAgent.chat.clientId.description: Optional client org ID scoping RAG memory recall/storage for the /agent command.

storyAgent.oauthProviders.description: OAuth providers for 'Story Agent: Connect OAuth Provider'. Merged over built-in defaults (aha, google, aws) by id. Each entry: { id, name, clientId, scopes[], and either discoveryUrl OR authorizationEndpoint+tokenEndpoint; optional clientSecret, resource }. Uses Authorization Code + PKCE with a localhost loopback redirect.
